enrichment.summary: Kyle does a casual life-update vlog from Boulder at age 20-something in 2014, prompted by a fan request. He covers recent events including buying a new MacBook Pro with his mom's help, getting part-time jobs as a janitor and soon as a snow shoveler, finding a rabid bat in his house, buying a motorcycle, and his ongoing work on his personal website. He reflects extensively on weed legalization in Colorado, audiobooks paired with labor jobs, frugal living, and his long-term plans for his website and YouTube videos.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: strongly pro-legalization; smokes heavily and sees weed as enhancing creativity and website ambition
labor jobs are ideal for audiobooks and can be used to self-educate out of lower economic classes
society should offer more jobs in one-hour increments for people who struggle with traditional full-time work
websites are primarily for organizing your own brain, not just for others; everything should be visible, never hidden in dropdowns
Ignatius Donnelly's Atlantis book is a slam dunk; ancient world mythologies are all the same story and deserve a well-organized webpage
not currently a priority because he's too focused on his website; doesn't care enough to make them right now
canceled it since biking to work and physical jobs provide sufficient exercise

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I just got a message from Shaman on Facebook saying he's my number one fan of all time on YouTube and that I should do an update video.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had a couple of hits on my oil pen and I'm doing this video because I'm bored.

claims.subject: Kyle's mom
claims.claim: My mom kept insisting I need an Apple computer because my brother got one and was impressed by it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't have the money for an Apple computer and I'm always on Dreamweaver, so I don't really need one.

claims.subject: Kyle's computer
claims.claim: My computer is a piece of shit and can't even play videos properly.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I ended up getting a refurbished Apple computer with my mom's help, spending $2500, with her contributing $700.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm paying $140 a month for the computer with no interest for a year.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My old computer was crashing all the time, and someone suggested reinstalling the operating system, but I never did.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I gave my old computer to the store for a data transfer and will pick up the new one tomorrow.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I found a bat in my house that tested positive for rabies, but it didn't bite me, so I didn't need vaccination.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got a part-time job as a janitor to make ends meet after miscalculating taxes and rent.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Being a janitor is a good job for listening to audiobooks and getting into a routine.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been thinking about being a janitor for years because it's a perfect job to focus on personal projects.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I also got a job as a snow shoveler for the winter, which will be another good opportunity to listen to audiobooks.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been smoking a lot of weed this year, staying high as much as possible.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Smoking weed helps me see the future steps of my website projects clearly.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't care about much when I'm high, which makes me focus intensely on my own projects.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I rode my motorcycle a few times this summer, including a trip to Rollinsville over St. Mary's Glacier.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to do more motorbiking next year and have ordered a new tire and lift for my motorcycle.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm working on a website project that involves organizing all ancient mythologies into one cohesive page, focusing on Atlantis.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to turn Ignatius Donnelly's book 'Atlantis: The Antediluvian World' into a webpage to prove the connections between global mythologies.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My website is designed to have everything visible at once without dropdown menus, which helps me organize information better.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I hope to finish my current website projects by the end of the year and then focus on the Atlantis page.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I estimate it will take me a year and a half to two years to finish my website and get out of debt.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might start making YouTube videos again once my website is more organized, but right now it's not a priority.
